title: DNA Methylation Signatures Predict HIV Prognosis and Mortality
author: Xinyu Zhang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.smdec26

steps.title: We conducted an EWAS meta-analysis by...
steps.instruction: We conducted an EWAS meta-analysis by combining the data from the discovery and replication samples. Effect size and p-values for each probe were obtained from analyses in cohort 1 and cohort 2 samples, respectively. We performed fixed-effects, inverse-variance meta-analysis, with scheme parameters of sample size and standard error by implementing the METAL (ver: 2010-02-08) program, combining summary statistics in two sample sets. We investigated heterogeneity in two sample sets using the I2-statistic.

steps.title: filtering of irrelevant probes based on methylation association analysis;
steps.instruction: filtering of irrelevant probes based on methylation association analysis;

steps.title: consideration of the relative attribution of...
steps.instruction: consideration of the relative attribution of each probe to rank the importance of the probes;

steps.title: application of multiple machine learning methods to optimize the model;
steps.instruction: application of multiple machine learning methods to optimize the model;

steps.title: building a model based on greedy...
steps.instruction: building a model based on greedy ensemble by applying a weighted average of 4 machine learning models;

steps.title: validation of the model in two independent samples
steps.instruction: validation of the model in two independent samples. Considering the samples were processed at different times and platforms, batch effects were removed using Function removeBatchEffect in R limma (ver. 3.32.

steps.title: library before performing machine learning prediction
steps.instruction: library before performing machine learning prediction. To reduce redundant DNA methylation signals and noise for improving the prediction accuracy of HIV frailty, CpG sites with FDR < 0.5 from EWAS in cohort 1 were selected for machine learning. The samples in cohort 1 were randomly divided into a training set and a test setwith ratio of 8:

steps.title: We first built a model using...
steps.instruction: We first built a model using the training set, in which each sample was labeled poor (VACS index >

steps.title: or good prognosis (VACS index <=50)
steps.instruction: or good prognosis (VACS index <=50). We then tested the model by performing 10-fold cross validation in the testing set, and the best-performed model was tested in an independent replication set.

steps.title: To examine whether the select CpG...
steps.instruction: To examine whether the select CpG site methylation was associated with mortality, we constructed a methylation index from the selected smoking-associated CpG sitesfollowing the previous formula[34]. A separate index was constructed for hypomethylated and hypermethylated CpG sites, respectively. The association of the DNA methylation risk index with all-cause mortality was examined by Kaplan–Meier plots and log-rank tests in all samples. Cox regression model was then used to adjust for age, antiretroviral therapy, HIV-1 loads, and CD4 counts. In the Cox regression model, the DNA methylation index score was a categorical variable (using the highest quartiles as the reference category) or a continuous variable (calculating HR for a decrease in DNA methylation by one standard deviation). Index hypo and index hyper were evaluated for the prediction of mortality separately.

steps.title: Pathway and network analysis was conducted...
steps.instruction: Pathway and network analysis was conducted for the selected CpG sites on the nearest genes by employing Ingenuity Pathway Analysis (IPA). For genes with multiple CpG sites, the lowest p-value at the CpG site within a gene was used to represent the gene level significance. Significant pathways were defined at a FDR <0.05.
